pairs.question: What were the early educational milestones of Cecil Herbert Bodington?
pairs.answer: Cecil Herbert Bodington began his education at a national school in Overstrand, Norfolk. In 1893, he secured a junior scholarship to Charterhouse School, where he studied for three years. He then progressed to The King's School, Canterbury, on a senior scholarship, and later matriculated to Peterhouse, Cambridge.

pairs.question: How did Cecil Bodington perform in first-class cricket for Hampshire?
pairs.answer: During his first-class cricket career for Hampshire in 1901 and 1902, Cecil Bodington played ten matches, scoring 154 runs at an average of exactly 11, with a highest score of 36. As a bowler, he took 9 wickets at an average of 31.88, with his best figures being 3 wickets for 19 runs.

pairs.question: What roles did Cecil Bodington have in education after graduating from Cambridge?
pairs.answer: After graduating from Cambridge, Cecil Bodington worked as a tutor to the three sons of the Maharaja of Kapurthala in British India. Upon returning to England, he became an assistant master at Elstree School and later at Stanmore Park Preparatory School.

pairs.question: What was Cecil Bodington's military career during World War I?
pairs.answer: Cecil Bodington served in the British Army during World War I. He was commissioned as a temporary second lieutenant in November 1914 and was assigned to the Household Battalion. He was promoted to temporary lieutenant in April 1916 and to temporary captain in September of the same year. He was killed in action on 11 April 1917 during the Battle of Arras.

pairs.question: Did Cecil Bodington play first-class cricket for Cambridge University?
pairs.answer: No, Cecil Bodington did not play first-class cricket for Cambridge University. However, he represented Hampshire in first-class cricket during his studies.

pairs.question: What were the dates of Cecil Herbert Bodington's birth and death?
pairs.answer: Cecil Herbert Bodington was born on 20 January 1880 and died on 11 April 1917.

pairs.question: How many wickets did Cecil Bodington take in first-class cricket, and what was his bowling average?
pairs.answer: Cecil Bodington took a total of 9 wickets in his first-class cricket career, with a bowling average of 31.88.
